hero.name: Fardin Zahid
hero.title: I make biology programmable!
hero.summary: Currently pursuing a Master’s degree in Biotechnologies and Applied Artificial Intelligence for Health at the University of Pisa. I aim to leverage machine learning and AI to uncover disease mechanisms and therapeutic responses for precision medicine.

education.degree: Master’s Degree, Biotechnologies and Applied Artificial Intelligence for Health
education.coursework: Bioinformatics, In-silico Modeling, AI, Probability and Biostatistics, OMICS, Bioengineering.
education.thesis: Cancer drug response modeling on cancer transcriptomics (ongoing) under Prof. Francesco Raimondi.

education.institution: Shahjalal University of Science and Technology, Bangladesh
education.degree: Bachelor’s Degree, Genetic Engineering and Biotechnology
education.thesis: Population genomics from 12 human populations: evidence from the past and implications for the future under Prof. Mohammad Zahangir Alam.

training.title: Metagenome in Newborns
training.description: Laboratory and computational training covering DNA extraction, PCR, electrophoresis, and sequencing analysis using Ubuntu, Conda, and QIIME2.

training.title: Next-Gen Pathogen Sequencing
training.description: Focused on infectious disease surveillance, pathogen genomics, clinical specimen processing, and sequencing on Illumina platforms.

training.title: Bioinformatics for Biologists
training.description: Managing large-scale biological datasets through Linux environments and automated pipelines using Python and R.

training.title: Scientific Computing with Python
training.description: Comprehensive hands-on training in Python programming, covering data structures, object-oriented programming, algorithms, and logic for scientific computing applications.

training.title: R for Reproducible Scientific Analysis
training.description: Training program teaching researchers how to write modular, reproducible code in R for statistical data analysis and visualization.

training.title: Drug Designing using Cheminformatics Techniques
training.description: Workshop covering the fundamentals of cheminformatics, molecular docking, and computational drug discovery workflows.

details: Building ML/AI models to predict drug response in cancer cell lines using gene-expression data.
Computationally identifying biomarkers and patterns that enhance biological interpretation.
Performed DNA/RNA extraction, purification, PCR, and RT-qPCR assays (COVID-19 and RSV).
Maintained microbial cell cultures, supporting experimental conditions.

experience.institution: Shahjalal University of Science and Technology
details: Conducted a population genomics study on 12 human populations from the 1000 Genomes Project.
Employed PLINK, PCA, F-statistics, Admixture analysis using KING-robust, SNPrelate, and R scripting.

awards.title: UNIPI Merit Scholarship
awards.description: Outstanding international student scholar at the University of Pisa.

awards.title: Talentpool Scholarship
awards.description: Top-tier merit scholarship by the Ministry of Education, Bangladesh.

conferences.title: Beyond the mechanism of action: a data-driven interpretable model reveals new determinants of cancer drug sensitivity
conferences.authors: Varisco, M., Zahid, F., Carli, F., Raimondi, F.
conferences.details: Research Late-Breaking Abstract (FEBS26-LB-RES-2711) - Topic: Cancer and therapy strategies.

publications.title: Communication Breakdown and Evolution of the Cancer Cell
publications.authors: Nemati Fard, L.A., Arora, C., Miglionico, P., Varisco, M., Bisceglia, L., Zahid, F., Vukotic, R., Raimondi, F.
publications.journal: Manuscript under review at Molecular Systems Biology.

publications.title: In silico investigations on curcuminoids from Curcuma longa as positive regulators of the Wnt/β-catenin signaling pathway in wound healing
publications.authors: Reshad, R.A.I., Alam, S., Raihan, H.B., Meem, K.N., Rahman, F., Zahid, F., Rafid, M.I., Rahman, S.M.O., Omit, S., & Ali, M.H.
publications.journal: Egyptian Journal of Medical Human Genetics, 22, 65.
